statement: US software developer employment will grow at least 3% YoY through 2026-Q2
rationale: AI augmentation raises output per developer; Jevons effect on software demand.
resolution_criteria: BLS occupational employment statistics for SOC 15-1252.

statement: Median cloud compute price per token will fall at least 40% by 2026-Q1 vs 2025-Q1
rationale: Hardware scaling and competition among frontier labs compress inference costs.
resolution_criteria: Public pricing pages from two major API providers.

statement: US paralegal headcount will decline at least 5% YoY by 2025-Q4
rationale: Document review automation substitutes routine legal research tasks.
resolution_criteria: BLS QCEW or OES release for legal support occupations.
judged_rationale: BLS data showed a 6.2% YoY decline in paralegal employment.

statement: US call-center employment will fall more than 8% in 2025
rationale: Voice AI handles tier-1 support; firms consolidate offshore and automated channels.
resolution_criteria: BLS occupational series for customer service representatives.
judged_rationale: Annual change exceeded the 8% threshold in federal labor statistics.

statement: Major economies will enter recession before 2025-Q3
rationale: Tight monetary policy and credit contraction trigger synchronized downturn.
resolution_criteria: NBER or equivalent two-quarter GDP contraction in US, EU, or UK.
judged_rationale: No G7 economy met the recession definition by the resolution date.

statement: Data-entry clerk employment will drop at least 10% YoY by 2025-Q4
rationale: OCR and LLM form extraction replace manual keying in back-office workflows.
resolution_criteria: BLS occupational employment for data entry keyers.
judged_rationale: Occupational series showed an 11% decline versus prior year.

statement: Venture capital AI funding will exceed $80B globally in 2025
rationale: Foundation-model capex and enterprise adoption sustain deal volume.
resolution_criteria: PitchBook or Crunchbase annual AI-sector funding totals.
judged_rationale: Reported annual totals remained below the $80B threshold.

statement: EU will pass binding AI employment impact reporting rules before 2026-Q2
rationale: AI Act implementation pushes member states toward workforce disclosure.
resolution_criteria: Official Journal publication of binding employment-reporting requirements.

statement: Combined 2024 capex of Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon and Meta will exceed $200B, driven by AI infrastructure
rationale: Frontier-model training and inference demand force a step-change in data-center buildout; all four guided sharply higher each quarter of 2024.
resolution_criteria: Sum of company-reported full-year 2024 capex from 10-K / annual filings.
judged_rationale: The four spent a combined ~$246-251B in 2024 (up 62% from 2023's ~$155B): Microsoft $75.6B, Amazon $83.9B, Google $52.5B, Meta $39.2B.

statement: NVIDIA full-year (FY2025) Data Center revenue will exceed $90B
rationale: GPU supply remains demand-constrained as hyperscalers and labs race to train larger models; Hopper/Blackwell ramp sustains record quarters.
resolution_criteria: NVIDIA fiscal-2025 annual results, Data Center segment revenue.
judged_rationale: NVIDIA reported FY2025 Data Center revenue of $115.2B, up 142% YoY, well above the $90B threshold.

statement: ChatGPT will surpass 600M weekly active users by the end of 2025
rationale: Consumer plus enterprise/developer adoption compounds; the product crossed 300M WAU in Dec 2024 and was still accelerating.
resolution_criteria: Public statement from OpenAI on weekly active users.
judged_rationale: OpenAI reported 700M WAU by Sept 2025 and 800M by Oct 2025, exceeding the 600M threshold.

statement: Frontier-class LLM API output price will fall at least 60% between Mar 2023 and Dec 2024
rationale: Hardware efficiency, model distillation and competition collapse inference prices for comparable-quality models.
resolution_criteria: Published per-million-token output price for OpenAI's flagship model, Mar 2023 vs Dec 2024.
judged_rationale: GPT-4 launched at $60/M output tokens (Mar 2023); GPT-4o was $10/M by Oct 2024 - an ~83% reduction at higher quality.

statement: US software developer employment will decline in 2024 as AI coding tools displace roles
rationale: Code-generation assistants automate routine programming; firms cut engineering headcount as output-per-developer rises.
resolution_criteria: BLS Occupational Outlook / OES employment level for software developers (SOC 15-1252), 2024 vs 2023.
judged_rationale: ROOT_CAUSE:wrong_granularity. Aggregate BLS employment (~1.9M, +15% outlook) did not decline — wrong metric. The real effect was concentrated in junior/entry-level and large-tech segments, not total headcount. Lesson: predictions about AI displacement must specify segment (junior vs senior, aggregate vs big-tech) and mechanism (layoffs vs hiring freeze), not just direction of total employment.

statement: Junior software developer hiring at large tech companies (>1000 employees) will fall at least 15% from 2022 peak levels by end of 2025
rationale: AI coding tools automate the boilerplate and bug-fix work that was historically the entry point for new-grad engineers; firms that over-hired in 2021-22 are letting junior cohorts shrink rather than backfilling attrition.
resolution_criteria: Job-posting trackers (Cadence/BLS) and staffing analyses showing junior SWE openings vs 2022 baseline at large tech employers.
judged_rationale: CONTRAST_PAIR_FOR:wrong_granularity_miss. Cadence/BLS data: junior SWE postings at large tech down ~22% at companies >1000 employees by 2025, down ~28-40% from 2022 peak overall. The real AI effect was real — but confined to the junior/entry segment, not aggregate headcount. Specifying the correct unit of analysis turns a MISS into a HIT.

statement: No major AI lab will publicly declare it has achieved human-level AGI by the end of 2025
rationale: Despite rapid capability gains, no agreed AGI benchmark exists and labs avoid the claim; the bar remains far from routine economic generality.
resolution_criteria: Absence of an official AGI-achieved claim from OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Anthropic, Meta or xAI.
judged_rationale: No major lab declared AGI by end-2025; frontier releases (GPT-5-class, Gemini, Claude) were framed as incremental capability gains, not AGI.

statement: Combined hyperscaler AI capex (MSFT, Alphabet, Amazon, Meta) will exceed $350B in 2025
rationale: 2025 guidance from all four implies a further step-up: Microsoft ~$80B+ FY, Amazon/Alphabet/Meta each guiding sharply higher.
resolution_criteria: Sum of company-reported full-year 2025 capex from annual filings.

statement: ChatGPT will exceed 1B weekly active users by the end of 2026
rationale: Growth trajectory (300M Dec-2024 to 900M Feb-2026) plus deeper OS/enterprise embedding points to continued compounding.
resolution_criteria: Public OpenAI statement on weekly active users (distinct from monthly active users).
